# AIOS Bible — Documentation Recheck & Suggestions

*Generated by the doc-integration verification + recheck pass over P0 (Brain), P1 (Institutions), P2 (Domains & Interfaces).*

## Summary

A deep recheck was performed on top of the structural verifier (`verify_docs.ps1`):

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| Structural verifier (DOC-FMT/ID/REF/LAW/DNA/CC/INV/STUB) | FAIL=24 (all P3 forward-refs), WARN=0 |
| Inline path-reference resolution (prose) | 0 broken in-scope refs |
| Placeholder / TODO / stub markers | 0 real (only false positives: "wiped", "Filled", "show placeholder" UI states) |
| Empty sections (sibling heading with no body) | 0 |
| Event-reference consistency (backticked `Comp.Event` resolves to a defined event) | 0 undefined |
| `Law N` mentions in prose (must be 1–10) | 0 out of range |

The 24 remaining FAILs are forward-references to P3 (Execution-tier) components
(`Bible/04-Execution/...`, `Bible/02-Core/{ROS,OSYS,DTS,Academy}`), which are out of P0/P1/P2 scope.

## Fixes applied this session

### 1. Event-name prefix convention standardized (was inconsistent)
- **Problem:** Event *definitions* (Events tables) had been normalized to the doc-ID
  abbreviation scheme (`LLM.*`, `SOU.*`, `VCE.*`, `BRN.*`), but *prose* references and a few
  un-touched tables still used full component names (`LLMOS.*`, `Sou.*`, `VOI.*`, `Brain.*`),
  and `LLMOS/013-Provider-SDK.md` was skipped entirely because its heading is
  `## Events (Provider-Level)`. This broke event cross-references.
- **Resolution:** All event identities now use the corpus's doc-ID component abbreviation as the
  prefix — `LLM, SOU, VCE, BRN, MSN, ORG, WRK, COG, CONV, MEM, CTX, PLN, DEC, TOL, ATT, PER,
  VIS, AUT, COD, COM, ECON, EMB, FPGA, LNX, RES, ROB, SEC, TRD, API, CON, DASH, SDK, UI`.
  Applied to both event-definition tables (incl. `## Events (...)` variants) and backticked
  prose references. After the fix, event-reference consistency = **0 undefined**.
- **Note:** `Physics/005-Events.md` defines *category* Types (`System`, `Interaction`,
  `Security`, `Mission`, `Resource`, `Evidence`) at a different level. Bible component-prefixed
  event identities are complementary, not conflicting.

### 2. `Law 13` → `R13` mislabel (real defect)
- `Bible/02-Core/Brain/Tools/005-Sandboxing.md:20` said *"Under Law 13 (Design for Failure)"*.
  Design for Failure is **Design DNA rule R13**, not a Physics Law (Physics defines only Law 1–10).
  Corrected to `R13 (Design for Failure)`.
- **Recommendation (corpus rule):** in prose, refer to Physics Laws as `Law N` (1–10) and to
  Design DNA rules as `R<N>`. The structural verifier only checks the `Source Laws` property
  field, not prose, so this class of error is currently caught only by the prose recheck.

### 3. Verifier bug fixed (DOC-CC-003)
- The verifier's first event-name regex captured only one group but joined `$Matches[1] + "." +
  $Matches[2]`, producing a spurious trailing dot on correct backticked events (e.g.
  `LLMOS.ProviderRegistered.`). Fixed to capture the whole `Component.Event` as one group.
  (This was a verifier false positive, not a doc defect.)

## Suggestions (not yet applied)

### S1. Replace placeholder example code `API_XXX`
- `Bible/08-Interfaces/API/000-Specifications.md:72` uses `"code": "API_XXX"` as a placeholder
  error code in an example payload.
- **Suggest:** replace with a concrete example code from the doc's own error table (e.g.
  `API_INVALID_REQUEST` or an existing `API_*` code) so the example is self-consistent.

### S2. Conceptual events in overview docs
- `Bible/02-Core/Brain/000-Overview.md` and `Bible/02-Core/Brain/Sou/000-Overview.md` reference
  conceptual events (`BRN.AuthorizationRequired`, `Sou.DecisionMade`, etc.) that are not defined
  in a dedicated Events table within those overview docs.
- **Suggest:** either add an Events table to each overview doc, or explicitly cross-reference the
  sub-doc that owns the event definition, so every referenced event has a discoverable definition.

### S3. Producer → consumer event traceability (deeper semantic check)
- The current checks confirm event *names* are consistent, but do not verify that every event
  "Produced By" a component is actually consumed/referenced by its intended subscriber doc
  (per `Physics/005` Invariant 6 — Evidence Is Actionable).
- **Suggest:** a future check that, for each defined event, confirms at least one subscriber doc
  (Observability, Audit, Academy, Security Council, Organizations, Notifications) references it.

### S4. Preserve Design DNA rule-range comment
- Docs such as `Bible/07-Domains/Embedded/001-Devices.md` originally used a `## Design DNA
  (R1-R6,R9,R10,R13-R15)` heading. This was normalized to `## Design DNA`. If the parenthetical
  rule-range is useful, keep it as a comment/`### Rule Coverage` note rather than in the heading.

## How to reproduce
- Structural: `verify_docs.ps1`
- Reference / placeholder / empty-section recheck: `recheck.ps1`
- Event + Law consistency recheck: `eventcheck.ps1`
- Event-prefix normalization: `fixevents.ps1`
- Design DNA / Cross-Cutting / Invariants remediation: `remediate.ps1`
- (All under `C:\Users\ncibi\AppData\Local\Temp\opencode\`.)

## Remaining out-of-scope work
- Author P3 (Execution-tier) docs to clear the 24 `DOC-REF-001` forward-reference FAILs.
